question_text: Explain why breaking a bar magnet in half creates two new complete magnets rather than a north pole piece and a south pole piece.
correct_answer: Each piece of the magnet contains atoms with aligned magnetic fields. When the magnet breaks, the atomic structure in each piece ensures that north and south poles form at the new surfaces.
explanation: Magnetism comes from aligned atomic/electron magnetic fields throughout the material. Breaking the magnet separates the material but doesn't stop the alignment of atoms, so each piece develops its own north and south poles.
